title: Callianthemum rutifolium
intro: Callianthemum rutifolium, also known as alpine blue gentian, is a perennial alpine plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing relief from digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Callianthemum rutifolium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Callianthemum rutifolium is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, which is used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Callianthemum rutifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and southwestern Canada. This plant is a perennial herb with distinctive white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Callianthemum rutifolium is a member of the genus Callianthemum, which is part of the order Gentianales. The plant's classification also includes its family and order designations as part of its overall taxonomic ranking.

constituents: Callianthemum rutifolium, also known as alpine beauty, contains a variety of active constituents. These include iridoid glycosides, specifically rutifolin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Rutifolin has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.
